# UC-10.16.13 · Symantec Endpoint Protection Blocked Malware by Risk and Host

## Description

Aggregating SEP risk events where the agent actively blocked, quarantined, or cleaned malware validates signature effectiveness and highlights persistent strains hitting specific machines.

## Value

Shows which malware families are most common in successful blocks and which hosts see repeat detections, guiding reimaging or policy tightening without waiting for weekly SEPM reports.

## Implementation

Deploy TA-sep14 (Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection) and ingest Client Risk logs from SEPM dumps or a supported forwarder path—avoid lossy UDP syslog for production. Map `Computer_Name` to CMDB. Tune `Actual_Action` values for your locale. Extend with threat intel lookups on `Risk_Name` or hash fields if extracted.

## SPL

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:risk:file" earliest=-24h
| eval action=coalesce(Actual_Action, actual_action, "")
| where action="Blocked" OR action="Quarantined" OR action="Cleaned"
| stats count by Risk_Name, Computer_Name, action
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (detections by Risk_Name), table (host × action), map or pie by action.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2772)
- [About the Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/AddOns/released/SymantecEP/Sourcetypes/)
